# The Gambia Malaria Budgeting Tool

A simple, browser-based tool for building, costing, visualising and comparing
multi-year malaria intervention budgets for The Gambia. It needs **no software
install, no spreadsheets, and no internet connection** to run.

## Use it online

Open the hosted link in any modern browser (Chrome, Edge, or Firefox):

> **<paste your GitHub Pages / Netlify link here>**

## Use it offline (no internet needed)

1. Unzip `gambia-malaria-budgeting-tool.zip` to any folder on your computer.
2. Double-click **`index.html`** — it opens in your web browser.
3. That's it. Everything runs inside your browser; nothing is uploaded anywhere.

> Use a modern browser (Chrome, Edge, or Firefox). The tool works fully offline.

## How it works (the 5 steps)

1. **Scenario specification** – choose interventions, where they run, and coverage.
2. **Cost specification** – review or adjust the unit costs (USD and GMD).
3. **Budget generation** – combine a scenario with a cost set to produce a budget.
4. **Budget visualisation** – explore one budget: charts, maps and tables.
5. **Budget comparison** – place budgets side by side against a baseline.

The **Overview** and **Methods** tabs explain, in plain language, what the tool
does and how the numbers are calculated.

## Good to know

- **Your work is saved in your own browser, on your own computer** — it is not
  shared between people or machines. To share results, use the **Export to Excel**
  buttons on the Generation, Visualisation and Comparison tabs.
- Clearing your browser data (or using private/incognito mode) will remove saved
  scenarios, cost sets and budgets.
- The tool contains **no personal or patient data** — only population, incidence
  and unit-cost reference figures.

## Questions

Built by PATH. For questions about the methods or assumptions, see the in-app
**Methods** tab.
